# terrible_check_point_mgmt_cp_mgmt_access_rule_facts (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_response` (Boolean) Run command in asynchronous mode and return task UID. Use show-task command to check the progress of the task. Available with customized HF.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `dereference_group_members` (Boolean) Indicates whether to dereference "members" field by details level for every object in reply. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `details_level` (String) The level of detail for some of the fields in the response can vary from showing only the UID value of the object to a fully detailed representation of the object.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `filter` (String) Search expression to filter the rulebase. The provided text should be exactly the same as it would be given in Smart Console. The logical operators in the expression ('AND', 'OR') should be provided in capital letters. If an operator is not used, the default OR operator applies.
- `filter_settings` (String) Sets filter preferences. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `hits_settings` (String) N/A
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `layer` (String) Layer that the rule belongs to identified by the name or UID.
- `limit` (Number) No more than that many results will be returned. This parameter is relevant only for getting few objects. Valid values are between 1 and 500.
- `name` (String) Object name. Should be unique in the domain.
- `offset` (Number) Skip that many results before beginning to return them. This parameter is relevant only for getting few objects.
- `order` (String) Sorts results by the given field. By default the results are sorted in the ascending order by name. This parameter is relevant only for getting few objects.
- `package` (String) Name of the package.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `show_as_ranges` (Boolean) When true, the source, destination and services & applications parameters are displayed as ranges of IP addresses and port numbers rather than network objects.<br /> Objects that are not represented using IP addresses or port numbers are presented as objects.<br /> In addition, the response of each rule does not contain the parameters, source, source-negate, destination, destination-negate, service and service-negate, but instead it contains the parameters, source-ranges, destination-ranges and service-ranges.<br /><br /> Note, Requesting to show rules as ranges is limited up to 20 rules per request, otherwise an error is returned. If you wish to request more rules, use the offset and limit parameters to limit your request. Available from R80.20 management version.
- `show_hits` (Boolean) N/A
- `show_membership` (Boolean) Indicates whether to calculate and show "groups" field for every object in reply. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `use_object_dictionary` (Boolean) N/A
- `wait_for_task` (Boolean) Wait for the task to end. Such as publish task.
- `wait_for_task_timeout` (Number) How many minutes to wait until throwing a timeout error.

### Read-Only

- `ansible_facts` (String) The checkpoint object facts.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
